title: FCoP Boundary & Capability
description: Abstraction 6 of 7 — Boundary. Defines the v1.0 capability vocabulary and the four boundary rules a runtime MUST enforce when a write op (write_task / write_report / write_review / archive_*) is dispatched. Per ADR-0020. Per ADR-0015 §abstractions, this is the POSIX-permissions analogue of FCoP. The vocabulary is hard-coded at 10 tokens for v1.0; v1.1 may extend additively.

capabilityToken.description: v1.0 capability vocabulary (per ADR-0020 §design-details). Frozen for v1.x. Meanings: file_io = read/write any file under workspace_dir; task_io = create TASK and reply with REPORT; modify_code = touch source files outside workspace_dir; review_decision = author a REVIEW with decision != null; approve_release = sign off on tagged release; escalate = re-route a task up the governance chain; spawn_agent = create new role entries in fcop.json; override = bypass another agent's REVIEW decision; archive = move TASK/REPORT/REVIEW into log/; mark_done = flip TASK status to done without producing a REPORT.

layerDefaults.description: Default capability bundles per layer (per ADR-0020 §design-details table). Implementations MAY consult this object when an Agent has no explicit can/cannot. Informational only — not enforced as schema.

boundaryRule.description: One of the four v1.0 boundary rules (per ADR-0020 §validation). Implementations MUST enforce all four when handling any write op.

vocabulary_version.description: Pinned at 1.0.0 for the entire FCoP v1.x line. Bumping requires a new ADR superseding ADR-0020.

tokens.description: The 10-token v1.0 capability vocabulary. Length is hard-pinned to detect accidental drift.

rules.description: The 4 v1.0 boundary rules (per ADR-0020). Length pinned to 4.
